segments.text: "After this Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and told him,
This is what the Lord, the God of Israel says, ‘Let my people go so they can hold a religious festival for me in the desert.’
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Who is this ‘Lord’ that I should listen to his request to let Israel go?
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Pharaoh replied.
I don't know the Lord and I certainly won't let Israel leave!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: The God of the Hebrews came to us,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: they added.
Please allow us to go a three-day journey into the desert and offer sacrifices to the Lord our God. Otherwise he'll kill us by disease or by the sword.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Moses and Aaron, why are you trying to take people away from their assigned labor?
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Pharaoh asked.
Get back to work!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: he ordered.
Look here,
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: he went on.
There are many of your people here in our country and you are preventing them from doing their assigned labor.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: "That very day he ordered the slave drivers and those in charge of the people:
Don't give them any more straw to make bricks like before. Have them go and collect the straw themselves.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: But still make them produce the same quantity of bricks as before. They're lazy people—that's why they're calling out, asking, ‘Please let us go and offer sacrifices to our god.’
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Make their work harder so they can get on with it and not pay any attention to all these lies!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: "So the slave drivers and those in charge went out and told the people,
This is what Pharaoh has ordered: ‘I won't give you any more straw.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Go and get straw for yourselves wherever you can find it, because your work quota won't be reduced.’
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: "So the people went all over Egypt collecting stubble for straw.
"The slave drivers kept on forcing them, saying,
You still have to do the same work as you did when you had straw provided!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: "They beat the Israelite supervisors they had put in charge, shouting at them,
Why haven't you met your quota of bricks as you did before?
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: "The Israelite supervisors went to Pharaoh to complain, saying,
Why are you treating us your servants like this?
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: You don't give us any straw but your slave drivers demand that we make bricks, and they beat us! Your people are treating us badly!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: No, you're just lazy, lazy people!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Pharaoh replied.
That's why you keep on asking, ‘Please let us go and offer sacrifices to the Lord.’
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Now get out of here and go back to work! You won't be given any straw but you'll still have to produce the full quota of bricks!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: "The Israelite supervisors realized they were in trouble when they were told,
You must not reduce the daily production of bricks.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: "They went over to Moses and Aaron who were waiting for them after their meeting with Pharaoh,
"and said,
May the Lord see what you have done and judge you accordingly! You have made us like a bad smell to Pharaoh and his officials. You have put a sword in their hands to kill us!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: "Moses went back to the Lord and complained,
Why have you caused all this trouble for your own people, Lord? Was this why you sent me?
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Ever since I went to Pharaoh to speak for you, he has been even harder on your people, and you haven't done anything to save them!
segments.speaker: Pharaoh
